title: A judge approves Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book settlement
friendlyTitle: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book settlement is approved
summary: A US federal judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement covering more than 482,000 books that Anthropic obtained through pirate sites for Claude training. The underlying ruling distinguished the use of books for model training from the way the copies were acquired, and other AI copyright cases remain unresolved.
friendlySummary: Anthropic will pay authors and publishers after using pirated copies of books to train Claude.
whyItMatters: The settlement increases the financial and legal consequences of poorly documented training-data acquisition. AI companies and businesses buying models should expect more scrutiny of data provenance, licensing and vendor assurances.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI companies need to know where training data came from and whether they had permission to use it.
